subject: Physics
title: Hawking Radiation
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Hawking Radiation.

hook.text: Stephen Hawking showed that black holes aren't completely black. They slowly evaporate by emitting faint radiation.

buildup.text: Quantum mechanics says particle-antiparticle pairs constantly pop into existence near the event horizon.

discovery.text: Sometimes one particle falls in while the other escapes. The escaping particle carries energy away from the black hole.

twist.text: Over incomprehensibly long times, black holes lose all their mass this way and evaporate entirely.

climax.text: A stellar black hole would take longer than the age of the universe—10^67 years—to evaporate. Patience required.

punchline.text: Even black holes die. Slowly. Very, very slowly.

quiz.question: What is Hawking radiation?
options: Faint radiation emitted by black holes due to quantum effects near the event horizon
Light trapped inside a black hole that slowly leaks out
X-rays from gas falling into a black hole
